{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e5e351be78b24f9fb0c840c4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Parser configuration module\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module provides comprehensive configuration options for controlling\n4\t//! parsing behavior across different parser implementations. It defines:\n5\t//!\n6\t//! - [`ParserConfig`] - Main configuration struct for all parsing options\n7\t//! - [`ParserMode`] - Strict vs lenient parsing modes\n8\t//! - [`TypeConstructor`] - Custom hooks for type construction\n9\t//! - Builder pattern for fluent configuration\n10\t\n11\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n12\tuse std::fmt;\n13\t\n14\t/// Parser execution mode\n15\t///\n16\t/// Defines the strictness level for parsing operations. Each mode has\n17\t/// specific behaviors for handling malformed input, unknown fields, and\n18\t/// type mismatches.\n19\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n20\tpub enum ParserMode {\n21\t    /// Strict mode - reject any malformed or unexpected input\n22\t    ///\n23\t    /// In strict mode:\n24\t    /// - Unknown fields cause parsing to fail\n25\t    /// - Type mismatches are errors (not coerced)\n26\t    /// - Duplicate keys are rejected\n27\t    /// - All syntax rules are enforced\n28\t    /// - Missing required fields cause errors\n29\t    Strict,\n30\t\n31\t    /// Lenient mode - attempt to recover from errors\n32\t    ///\n33\t    /// In lenient mode:\n34\t    /// - Unknown fields are silently ignored\n35\t    /// - Type mismatches are coerced when possible (e.g., string → number)\n36\t    /// - Last duplicate key wins (with warning if configured)\n37\t    /// - Some syntax variations are accepted\n38\t    /// - Missing optional fields use defaults\n39\t    Lenient,\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\timpl ParserMode {\n43\t    /// Check if this mode is strict\n44\t    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool {\n45\t        matches!(self, Self::Strict)\n46\t    }\n47\t\n48\t    /// Check if this mode is lenient\n49\t    pub fn is_lenient(&self) -> bool {\n50\t        matches!(self, Self::Lenient)\n51\t    }\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\timpl fmt::Display for ParserMode {\n55\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n56\t        match self {\n57\t            Self::Strict => write!(f, \"strict\"),\n58\t            Self::Lenient => write!(f, \"lenient\"),\n59\t        }\n60\t    }\n61\t}\n62\t\n63\timpl Default for ParserMode {\n64\t    fn default() -> Self {\n65\t        // Default to lenient mode for better user experience\n66\t        Self::Lenient\n67\t    }\n68\t}\n69\t\n70\t/// Type constructor function signature\n71\t///\n72\t/// This function type defines the signature for custom type constructors.\n73\t/// It takes a field name and raw value, and attempts to construct the\n74\t/// target type from those inputs.\n75\tpub type TypeConstructorFn = fn(&str, &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String>;\n76\t\n77\t/// Custom type constructor hook\n78\t///\n79\t/// Allows users to register custom logic for constructing specific types\n80\t/// during parsing. This is useful for:\n81\t///\n82\t/// - Custom enum parsing (e.g., \"warn\" → LogLevel::Warning)\n83\t/// - Validation-rich construction (e.g., ensure ports are in range)\n84\t/// - Complex type assembly (e.g., Duration from \"5s\" string)\n85\t/// - Default value injection for optional fields\n86\t///\n87\t/// # Examples\n88\t///\n89\t/// ```\n90\t/// use armor::parsers::config::TypeConstructor;\n91\t///\n92\t/// // Custom constructor for log levels\n93\t/// fn log_level_constructor(\n94\t///     field: &str,\n95\t///     value: &serde_yaml::Value\n96\t/// ) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String> {\n97\t///     let s = value.as_str()\n98\t///         .ok_or(\"expected string\")?\n99\t///         .to_lowercase();\n100\t///\n101\t///     let level = match s.as_str() {\n102\t///         \"debug\" => 0,\n103\t///         \"info\" => 1,\n104\t///         \"warn\" | \"warning\" => 2,\n105\t///         \"error\" => 3,\n106\t///         _ => return Err(format!(\"invalid log level: {}\", s)),\n107\t///     };\n108\t///\n109\t///     Ok(serde_yaml::Value::Number(level.into()))\n110\t/// }\n111\t///\n112\t/// let constructor = TypeConstructor::new(\"LogLevel\", log_level_constructor);\n113\t/// ```\n114\t#[derive(Clone)]\n115\tpub struct TypeConstructor {\n116\t    /// Name of the type this constructor handles (for debugging)\n117\t    pub type_name: String,\n118\t    /// The constructor function\n119\t    pub constructor: TypeConstructorFn,\n120\t}\n121\t\n122\timpl TypeConstructor {\n123\t    /// Create a new type constructor\n124\t    ///\n125\t    /// # Arguments\n126\t    ///\n127\t    /// * `type_name` - Human-readable name for the type being constructed\n128\t    /// * `constructor` - Function that implements the construction logic\n129\t    pub fn new(type_name: impl Into<String>, constructor: TypeConstructorFn) -> Self {\n130\t        Self {\n131\t            type_name: type_name.into(),\n132\t            constructor,\n133\t        }\n134\t    }\n135\t\n136\t    /// Invoke the constructor\n137\t    ///\n138\t    /// Calls the underlying constructor function with the provided inputs.\n139\t    ///\n140\t    /// # Arguments\n141\t    ///\n142\t    /// * `field` - Field name being constructed\n143\t    /// * `value` - Raw value from the parser\n144\t    ///\n145\t    /// # Returns\n146\t    ///\n147\t    /// * `Ok(serde_yaml::Value)` - Successfully constructed value\n148\t    /// * `Err(String)` - Construction error message\n149\t    pub fn construct(&self, field: &str, value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String> {\n150\t        (self.constructor)(field, value)\n151\t    }\n152\t}\n153\t\n154\timpl fmt::Debug for TypeConstructor {\n155\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n156\t        f.debug_struct(\"TypeConstructor\")\n157\t            .field(\"type_name\", &self.type_name)\n158\t            .field(\"constructor\", &\"<function>\")\n159\t            .finish()\n160\t    }\n161\t}\n162\t\n163\t/// Validation hook function signature\n164\t///\n165\t/// This function type defines the signature for custom validation hooks.\n166\t/// It takes a field name and parsed value, and validates that the value\n167\t/// meets application-specific constraints.\n168\tpub type ValidationFn = fn(&str, &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<(), String>;\n169\t\n170\t/// Custom validation hook\n171\t///\n172\t/// Allows users to register custom validation logic for specific fields\n173\t/// or types. Validation runs after parsing but before the final result\n174\t/// is returned.\n175\t///\n176\t/// # Examples\n177\t///\n178\t/// ```\n179\t/// use armor::parsers::config::ValidationHook;\n180\t///\n181\t/// // Validate port numbers are in valid range\n182\t/// fn validate_port(\n183\t///     field: &str,\n184\t///     value: &serde_yaml::Value\n185\t/// ) -> Result<(), String> {\n186\t///     let port = value.as_i64()\n187\t///         .ok_or(\"port must be an integer\")?;\n188\t///\n189\t///     if !(1..=65535).contains(&port) {\n190\t///         return Err(format!(\"port {} out of valid range (1-65535)\", port));\n191\t///     }\n192\t///\n193\t///     Ok(())\n194\t/// }\n195\t///\n196\t/// let hook = ValidationHook::new(\"port\", validate_port);\n197\t/// ```\n198\t#[derive(Clone)]\n199\tpub struct ValidationHook {\n200\t    /// Pattern for matching field names (supports simple \"*\"\" wildcard)\n201\t    pub field_pattern: String,\n202\t    /// The validation function\n203\t    pub validator: ValidationFn,\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\timpl ValidationHook {\n207\t    /// Create a new validation hook\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// # Arguments\n210\t    ///\n211\t    /// * `field_pattern` - Pattern to match field names (supports \"*\"\" wildcard)\n212\t    /// * `validator` - Function that implements the validation logic\n213\t    pub fn new(field_pattern: impl Into<String>, validator: ValidationFn) -> Self {\n214\t        Self {\n215\t            field_pattern: field_pattern.into(),\n216\t            validator,\n217\t        }\n218\t    }\n219\t\n220\t    /// Check if this hook applies to the given field\n221\t    ///\n222\t    /// # Arguments\n223\t    ///\n224\t    /// * `field` - Field name to check\n225\t    ///\n226\t    /// # Returns\n227\t    ///\n228\t    /// `true` if the field pattern matches, `false` otherwise\n229\t    pub fn applies_to(&self, field: &str) -> bool {\n230\t        if self.field_pattern == \"*\" {\n231\t            return true;\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        if self.field_pattern.ends_with('*') {\n235\t            let prefix = &self.field_pattern[..self.field_pattern.len() - 1];\n236\t            return field.starts_with(prefix);\n237\t        }\n238\t\n239\t        self.field_pattern == field\n240\t    }\n241\t\n242\t    /// Invoke the validator\n243\t    ///\n244\t    /// Calls the underlying validation function with the provided inputs.\n245\t    ///\n246\t    /// # Arguments\n247\t    ///\n248\t    /// * `field` - Field name being validated\n249\t    /// * `value` - Parsed value to validate\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// # Returns\n252\t    ///\n253\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Validation passed\n254\t    /// * `Err(String)` - Validation error message\n255\t    pub fn validate(&self, field: &str, value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<(), String> {\n256\t        (self.validator)(field, value)\n257\t    }\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\timpl fmt::Debug for ValidationHook {\n261\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n262\t        f.debug_struct(\"ValidationHook\")\n263\t            .field(\"field_pattern\", &self.field_pattern)\n264\t            .field(\"validator\", &\"<function>\")\n265\t            .finish()\n266\t    }\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t/// Comprehensive parser configuration\n270\t///\n271\t/// This structure consolidates all configuration options for parsing behavior.\n272\t/// It provides fine-grained control over parsing, validation, and type construction.\n273\t///\n274\t/// # Default Configuration\n275\t///\n276\t/// The default configuration is:\n277\t///\n278\t/// ```ignore\n279\t/// ParserConfig {\n280\t///     mode: Lenient,\n281\t///     allow_duplicates: true,\n282\t///     preserve_comments: false,\n283\t///     preserve_quotes: false,\n284\t///     max_depth: 0,  // unlimited\n285\t///     strict_types: false,\n286\t///     .. }\n287\t/// ```\n288\t///\n289\t/// # Examples\n290\t///\n291\t/// ## Using Defaults\n292\t///\n293\t/// ```\n294\t/// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n295\t///\n296\t/// let config = ParserConfig::default();\n297\t/// ```\n298\t///\n299\t/// ## Strict Mode\n300\t///\n301\t/// ```\n302\t/// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n303\t///\n304\t/// let config = ParserConfig::strict();\n305\t/// ```\n306\t///\n307\t/// ## Builder Pattern\n308\t///\n309\t/// ```ignore\n310\t/// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, ParserMode};\n311\t///\n312\t/// let config = ParserConfig::builder()\n313\t///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n314\t///     .allow_duplicates(false)\n315\t///     .max_depth(10)\n316\t///     .build();\n317\t/// ```\n318\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n319\tpub struct ParserConfig {\n320\t    /// Parsing mode (strict vs lenient)\n321\t    pub mode: ParserMode,\n322\t    /// Allow duplicate keys in mappings\n323\t    pub allow_duplicates: bool,\n324\t    /// Preserve comments in the output (if format supports it)\n325\t    pub preserve_comments: bool,\n326\t    /// Preserve quote information in parsed strings\n327\t    pub preserve_quotes: bool,\n328\t    /// Preserve order of mapping keys (use index-based map instead of hash map)\n329\t    pub preserve_order: bool,\n330\t    /// Maximum nesting depth (0 = unlimited)\n331\t    pub max_depth: usize,\n332\t    /// Enforce strict type checking (no implicit coercion)\n333\t    pub strict_types: bool,\n334\t    /// Custom type constructors registered by field name\n335\t    pub type_constructors: HashMap<String, TypeConstructor>,\n336\t    /// Custom validation hooks\n337\t    pub validation_hooks: Vec<ValidationHook>,\n338\t    /// Emit warnings for recoverable errors\n339\t    pub emit_warnings: bool,\n340\t    /// Treat warnings as errors (fail on warnings)\n341\t    pub warnings_as_errors: bool,\n342\t}\n343\t\n344\timpl Default for ParserConfig {\n345\t    fn default() -> Self {\n346\t        Self {\n347\t            mode: ParserMode::default(),\n348\t            allow_duplicates: true,\n349\t            preserve_comments: false,\n350\t            preserve_quotes: false,\n351\t            preserve_order: false,\n352\t            max_depth: 0,\n353\t            strict_types: false,\n354\t            type_constructors: HashMap::new(),\n355\t            validation_hooks: Vec::new(),\n356\t            emit_warnings: false,\n357\t            warnings_as_errors: false,\n358\t        }\n359\t    }\n360\t}\n361\t\n362\timpl ParserConfig {\n363\t    /// Create a builder for constructing ParserConfig\n364\t    ///\n365\t    /// # Examples\n366\t    ///\n367\t    /// ```\n368\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, ParserMode};\n369\t    ///\n370\t    /// let config = ParserConfig::builder()\n371\t    ///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n372\t    ///     .build();\n373\t    /// ```\n374\t    pub fn builder() -> ParserConfigBuilder {\n375\t        ParserConfigBuilder::new()\n376\t    }\n377\t\n378\t    /// Create a strict-mode configuration\n379\t    ///\n380\t    /// This is a convenience method that creates a configuration optimized\n381\t    /// for strict validation. Equivalent to:\n382\t    ///\n383\t    /// ```ignore\n384\t    /// ParserConfig::builder()\n385\t    ///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n386\t    ///     .allow_duplicates(false)\n387\t    ///     .strict_types(true)\n388\t    ///     .build()\n389\t    /// ```\n390\t    ///\n391\t    /// # Examples\n392\t    ///\n393\t    /// ```\n394\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n395\t    ///\n396\t    /// let config = ParserConfig::strict();\n397\t    /// assert!(config.mode.is_strict());\n398\t    /// assert!(!config.allow_duplicates);\n399\t    /// assert!(config.strict_types);\n400\t    /// ```\n401\t    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n402\t        Self {\n403\t            mode: ParserMode::Strict,\n404\t            allow_duplicates: false,\n405\t            strict_types: true,\n406\t            ..Default::default()\n407\t        }\n408\t    }\n409\t\n410\t    /// Create a lenient-mode configuration\n411\t    ///\n412\t    /// This is a convenience method that creates a configuration optimized\n413\t    /// for forgiving parsing. Equivalent to:\n414\t    ///\n415\t    /// ```ignore\n416\t    /// ParserConfig::builder()\n417\t    ///     .mode(ParserMode::Lenient)\n418\t    ///     .allow_duplicates(true)\n419\t    ///     .strict_types(false)\n420\t    ///     .build()\n421\t    /// ```\n422\t    ///\n423\t    /// # Examples\n424\t    ///\n425\t    /// ```\n426\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n427\t    ///\n428\t    /// let config = ParserConfig::lenient();\n429\t    /// assert!(config.mode.is_lenient());\n430\t    /// assert!(config.allow_duplicates);\n431\t    /// assert!(!config.strict_types);\n432\t    /// ```\n433\t    pub fn lenient() -> Self {\n434\t        Self {\n435\t            mode: ParserMode::Lenient,\n436\t            allow_duplicates: true,\n437\t            strict_types: false,\n438\t            ..Default::default()\n439\t        }\n440\t    }\n441\t\n442\t    /// Check if strict mode is enabled\n443\t    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool {\n444\t        self.mode.is_strict()\n445\t    }\n446\t\n447\t    /// Check if lenient mode is enabled\n448\t    pub fn is_lenient(&self) -> bool {\n449\t        self.mode.is_lenient()\n450\t    }\n451\t\n452\t    /// Register a custom type constructor\n453\t    ///\n454\t    /// # Arguments\n455\t    ///\n456\t    /// * `field` - Field name this constructor handles\n457\t    /// * `constructor` - The type constructor to register\n458\t    ///\n459\t    /// # Examples\n460\t    ///\n461\t    /// ```\n462\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, TypeConstructor};\n463\t    ///\n464\t    /// fn make_duration(_: &str, v: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String> {\n465\t    ///     // Implementation...\n466\t    ///     Ok(v.clone())\n467\t    /// }\n468\t    ///\n469\t    /// let mut config = ParserConfig::default();\n470\t    /// config.register_constructor(\"timeout\", TypeConstructor::new(\"Duration\", make_duration));\n471\t    /// ```\n472\t    pub fn register_constructor(&mut self, field: impl Into<String>, constructor: TypeConstructor) {\n473\t        self.type_constructors.insert(field.into(), constructor);\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    /// Register a custom validation hook\n477\t    ///\n478\t    /// # Arguments\n479\t    ///\n480\t    /// * `hook` - The validation hook to register\n481\t    ///\n482\t    /// # Examples\n483\t    ///\n484\t    /// ```\n485\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, ValidationHook};\n486\t    ///\n487\t    /// fn validate_port(field: &str, v: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<(), String> {\n488\t    ///     // Implementation...\n489\t    ///     Ok(())\n490\t    /// }\n491\t    ///\n492\t    /// let mut config = ParserConfig::default();\n493\t    /// config.register_validation(ValidationHook::new(\"port\", validate_port));\n494\t    /// ```\n495\t    pub fn register_validation(&mut self, hook: ValidationHook) {\n496\t        self.validation_hooks.push(hook);\n497\t    }\n498\t\n499\t    /// Get type constructor for a field (if registered)\n500\t    ///\n501\t    /// # Arguments\n502\t    ///\n503\t    /// * `field` - Field name to look up\n504\t    ///\n505\t    /// # Returns\n506\t    ///\n507\t    /// `Some(&TypeConstructor)` if registered, `None` otherwise\n508\t    pub fn get_constructor(&self, field: &str) -> Option<&TypeConstructor> {\n509\t        self.type_constructors.get(field)\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    /// Get all applicable validation hooks for a field\n513\t    ///\n514\t    /// # Arguments\n515\t    ///\n516\t    /// * `field` - Field name to check\n517\t    ///\n518\t    /// # Returns\n519\t    ///\n520\t    /// Iterator over all validation hooks that apply to this field\n521\t    pub fn get_validations<'a>(&'a self, field: &'a str) -> impl Iterator<Item = &'a ValidationHook> {\n522\t        self.validation_hooks\n523\t            .iter()\n524\t            .filter(move |hook| hook.applies_to(field))\n525\t    }\n526\t\n527\t    /// Validate configuration for mutually exclusive or inconsistent options\n528\t    ///\n529\t    /// This method checks if any mutually exclusive options are set together\n530\t    /// or if the configuration has inconsistent settings. Returns an error if\n531\t    /// validation fails.\n532\t    ///\n533\t    /// # Returns\n534\t    ///\n535\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Configuration is valid\n536\t    /// * `Err(String)` - Configuration has conflicting or inconsistent options\n537\t    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n538\t        // Check for mutually exclusive or inconsistent options\n539\t        if self.warnings_as_errors && !self.emit_warnings {\n540\t            return Err(\"warnings_as_errors requires emit_warnings to be true\".to_string());\n541\t        }\n542\t\n543\t        // Strict mode should not allow duplicates\n544\t        if self.mode.is_strict() && self.allow_duplicates {\n545\t            return Err(\"Strict mode with allow_duplicates=true is inconsistent\".to_string());\n546\t        }\n547\t\n548\t        // Strict types should align with strict mode\n549\t        if self.strict_types && self.mode.is_lenient() {\n550\t            return Err(\"strict_types=true with lenient mode is inconsistent\".to_string());\n551\t        }\n552\t\n553\t        Ok(())\n554\t    }\n555\t}\n556\t\n557\t/// Builder for constructing ParserConfig instances\n558\t///\n559\t/// Provides a fluent interface for creating parser configurations.\n560\t/// Use [`ParserConfig::builder()`] to create an instance.\n561\t///\n562\t/// # Examples\n563\t///\n564\t/// ```\n565\t/// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, ParserMode};\n566\t///\n567\t/// let config = ParserConfig::builder()\n568\t///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n569\t///     .allow_duplicates(false)\n570\t///     .max_depth(10)\n571\t///     .preserve_comments(true)\n572\t///     .build();\n573\t/// ```\n574\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n575\tpub struct ParserConfigBuilder {\n576\t    config: ParserConfig,\n577\t}\n578\t\n579\timpl ParserConfigBuilder {\n580\t    /// Create a new builder with default configuration\n581\t    fn new() -> Self {\n582\t        Self {\n583\t            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n584\t        }\n585\t    }\n586\t\n587\t    /// Set the parsing mode\n588\t    pub fn mode(mut self, mode: ParserMode) -> Self {\n589\t        self.config.mode = mode;\n590\t        self\n591\t    }\n592\t\n593\t    /// Set whether to allow duplicate keys\n594\t    pub fn allow_duplicates(mut self, allow: bool) -> Self {\n595\t        self.config.allow_duplicates = allow;\n596\t        self\n597\t    }\n598\t\n599\t    /// Set whether to preserve comments\n600\t    pub fn preserve_comments(mut self, preserve: bool) -> Self {\n601\t        self.config.preserve_comments = preserve;\n602\t        self\n603\t    }\n604\t\n605\t    /// Set whether to preserve quote information\n606\t    pub fn preserve_quotes(mut self, preserve: bool) -> Self {\n607\t        self.config.preserve_quotes = preserve;\n608\t        self\n609\t    }\n610\t\n611\t    /// Set whether to preserve mapping key order\n612\t    pub fn preserve_order(mut self, preserve: bool) -> Self {\n613\t        self.config.preserve_order = preserve;\n614\t        self\n615\t    }\n616\t\n617\t    /// Set maximum nesting depth\n618\t    pub fn max_depth(mut self, depth: usize) -> Self {\n619\t        self.config.max_depth = depth;\n620\t        self\n621\t    }\n622\t\n623\t    /// Set strict type checking\n624\t    pub fn strict_types(mut self, strict: bool) -> Self {\n625\t        self.config.strict_types = strict;\n626\t        self\n627\t    }\n628\t\n629\t    /// Add a type constructor\n630\t    pub fn with_constructor(mut self, field: impl Into<String>, constructor: TypeConstructor) -> Self {\n631\t        self.config.register_constructor(field, constructor);\n632\t        self\n633\t    }\n634\t\n635\t    /// Add a validation hook\n636\t    pub fn with_validation(mut self, hook: ValidationHook) -> Self {\n637\t        self.config.register_validation(hook);\n638\t        self\n639\t    }\n640\t\n641\t    /// Set whether to emit warnings\n642\t    pub fn emit_warnings(mut self, emit: bool) -> Self {\n643\t        self.config.emit_warnings = emit;\n644\t        self\n645\t    }\n646\t\n647\t    /// Set whether to treat warnings as errors\n648\t    pub fn warnings_as_errors(mut self, treat_as_errors: bool) -> Self {\n649\t        self.config.warnings_as_errors = treat_as_errors;\n650\t        self\n651\t    }\n652\t\n653\t    /// Build the final ParserConfig\n654\t    ///\n655\t    /// This method validates the configuration before returning it.\n656\t    ///\n657\t    /// # Returns\n658\t    ///\n659\t    /// * `Ok(ParserConfig)` - Valid configuration\n660\t    /// * `Err(String)` - Configuration validation failed\n661\t    pub fn build(self) -> Result<ParserConfig, String> {\n662\t        self.config.validate()?;\n663\t        Ok(self.config)\n664\t    }\n665\t\n666\t    /// Build without validation (internal use only)\n667\t    #[doc(hidden)]\n668\t    pub fn build_unchecked(self) -> ParserConfig {\n669\t        self.config\n670\t    }\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\t/// Validation mode for field checking\n674\t///\n675\t/// Defines the strictness level for validation operations. Each mode has\n676\t/// specific behaviors for field presence and type checking.\n677\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n678\tpub enum ValidationMode {\n679\t    /// Strict mode - enforce all validation rules\n680\t    ///\n681\t    /// In strict mode:\n682\t    /// - All required fields must be present\n683\t    /// - Unknown fields cause validation to fail\n684\t    /// - Type mismatches are errors\n685\t    Strict,\n686\t\n687\t    /// Permissive mode - allow some flexibility\n688\t    ///\n689\t    /// In permissive mode:\n690\t    /// - Required fields use defaults if missing\n691\t    /// - Unknown fields are ignored with warnings\n692\t    /// - Type mismatches are coerced when possible\n693\t    Permissive,\n694\t}\n695\t\n696\timpl ValidationMode {\n697\t    /// Check if this mode is strict\n698\t    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool {\n699\t        matches!(self, Self::Strict)\n700\t    }\n701\t\n702\t    /// Check if this mode is permissive\n703\t    pub fn is_permissive(&self) -> bool {\n704\t        matches!(self, Self::Permissive)\n705\t    }\n706\t}\n707\t\n708\timpl fmt::Display for ValidationMode {\n709\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n710\t        match self {\n711\t            Self::Strict => write!(f, \"strict\"),\n712\t            Self::Permissive => write!(f, \"permissive\"),\n713\t        }\n714\t    }\n715\t}\n716\t\n717\timpl Default for ValidationMode {\n718\t    fn default() -> Self {\n719\t        Self::Permissive\n720\t    }\n721\t}\n722\t\n723\t/// Comprehensive validator configuration\n724\t///\n725\t/// This structure consolidates all configuration options for validation behavior.\n726\t/// It provides fine-grained control over field checking, type validation, and\n727\t/// constraint enforcement.\n728\t///\n729\t/// # Default Configuration\n730\t///\n731\t/// The default configuration is:\n732\t///\n733\t/// ```ignore\n734\t/// ValidatorConfig {\n735\t///     mode: Permissive,\n736\t///     require_all_fields: false,\n737\t///     disallow_unknown_fields: false,\n738\t///     type_checking: true,\n739\t///     .. }\n740\t/// ```\n741\t///\n742\t/// # Examples\n743\t///\n744\t/// ## Using Defaults\n745\t///\n746\t/// ```\n747\t/// use armor::parsers::config::ValidatorConfig;\n748\t///\n749\t/// let config = ValidatorConfig::default();\n750\t/// ```\n751\t///\n752\t/// ## Strict Mode\n753\t///\n754\t/// ```\n755\t/// use armor::parsers::config::ValidatorConfig;\n756\t///\n757\t/// let config = ValidatorConfig::strict();\n758\t/// ```\n759\t///\n760\t/// ## Builder Pattern\n761\t///\n762\t/// ```ignore\n763\t/// use armor::parsers::config::{ValidatorConfig, ValidationMode};\n764\t///\n765\t/// let config = ValidatorConfig::builder()\n766\t///     .mode(ValidationMode::Strict)\n767\t///     .require_all_fields(true)\n768\t///     .disallow_unknown_fields(true)\n769\t///     .build();\n770\t/// ```\n771\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n772\tpub struct ValidatorConfig {\n773\t    /// Validation mode (strict vs permissive)\n774\t    pub mode: ValidationMode,\n775\t    /// Require all fields to be present (no missing fields)\n776\t    pub require_all_fields: bool,\n777\t    /// Disallow unknown fields (fail on unexpected fields)\n778\t    pub disallow_unknown_fields: bool,\n779\t    /// Enable type checking (validate field types match schema)\n780\t    pub type_checking: bool,\n781\t    /// Perform deep validation on nested structures\n782\t    pub deep_validation: bool,\n783\t    /// Emit warnings for non-critical issues\n784\t    pub emit_warnings: bool,\n785\t    /// Treat warnings as errors (fail on warnings)\n786\t    pub warnings_as_errors: bool,\n787\t}\n788\t\n789\timpl Default for ValidatorConfig {\n790\t    fn default() -> Self {\n791\t        Self {\n792\t            mode: ValidationMode::default(),\n793\t            require_all_fields: false,\n794\t            disallow_unknown_fields: false,\n795\t            type_checking: true,\n796\t            deep_validation: true,\n797\t            emit_warnings: true,\n798\t            warnings_as_errors: false,\n799\t        }\n800\t    }\n801\t}\n802\t\n803\timpl ValidatorConfig {\n804\t    /// Create a builder for constructing ValidatorConfig\n805\t    ///\n806\t    /// # Examples\n807\t    ///\n808\t    /// ```\n809\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::{ValidatorConfig, ValidationMode};\n810\t    ///\n811\t    /// let config = ValidatorConfig::builder()\n812\t    ///     .mode(ValidationMode::Strict)\n813\t    ///     .build();\n814\t    /// ```\n815\t    pub fn builder() -> ValidatorConfigBuilder {\n816\t        ValidatorConfigBuilder::new()\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    /// Create a strict-mode configuration\n820\t    ///\n821\t    /// This is a convenience method that creates a configuration optimized\n822\t    /// for strict validation. Equivalent to:\n823\t    ///\n824\t    /// ```ignore\n825\t    /// ValidatorConfig::builder()\n826\t    ///     .mode(ValidationMode::Strict)\n827\t    ///     .require_all_fields(true)\n828\t    ///     .disallow_unknown_fields(true)\n829\t    ///     .type_checking(true)\n830\t    ///     .build()\n831\t    /// ```\n832\t    ///\n833\t    /// # Examples\n834\t    ///\n835\t    /// ```\n836\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::ValidatorConfig;\n837\t    ///\n838\t    /// let config = ValidatorConfig::strict();\n839\t    /// assert!(config.mode.is_strict());\n840\t    /// assert!(config.require_all_fields);\n841\t    /// assert!(config.disallow_unknown_fields);\n842\t    /// assert!(config.type_checking);\n843\t    /// ```\n844\t    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n845\t        Self {\n846\t            mode: ValidationMode::Strict,\n847\t            require_all_fields: true,\n848\t            disallow_unknown_fields: true,\n849\t            type_checking: true,\n850\t            ..Default::default()\n851\t        }\n852\t    }\n853\t\n854\t    /// Create a permissive-mode configuration\n855\t    ///\n856\t    /// This is a convenience method that creates a configuration optimized\n857\t    /// for flexible validation. Equivalent to:\n858\t    ///\n859\t    /// ```ignore\n860\t    /// ValidatorConfig::builder()\n861\t    ///     .mode(ValidationMode::Permissive)\n862\t    ///     .require_all_fields(false)\n863\t    ///     .disallow_unknown_fields(false)\n864\t    ///     .type_checking(true)\n865\t    ///     .build()\n866\t    /// ```\n867\t    ///\n868\t    /// # Examples\n869\t    ///\n870\t    /// ```\n871\t    /// use armor::parsers::config::ValidatorConfig;\n872\t    ///\n873\t    /// let config = ValidatorConfig::permissive();\n874\t    /// assert!(config.mode.is_permissive());\n875\t    /// assert!(!config.require_all_fields);\n876\t    /// assert!(!config.disallow_unknown_fields);\n877\t    /// assert!(config.type_checking);\n878\t    /// ```\n879\t    pub fn permissive() -> Self {\n880\t        Self {\n881\t            mode: ValidationMode::Permissive,\n882\t            require_all_fields: false,\n883\t            disallow_unknown_fields: false,\n884\t            type_checking: true,\n885\t            ..Default::default()\n886\t        }\n887\t    }\n888\t\n889\t    /// Check if strict mode is enabled\n890\t    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool {\n891\t        self.mode.is_strict()\n892\t    }\n893\t\n894\t    /// Check if permissive mode is enabled\n895\t    pub fn is_permissive(&self) -> bool {\n896\t        self.mode.is_permissive()\n897\t    }\n898\t\n899\t    /// Validate configuration for mutually exclusive options\n900\t    ///\n901\t    /// This method checks if any mutually exclusive options are set together.\n902\t    /// Returns an error if validation fails.\n903\t    ///\n904\t    /// # Returns\n905\t    ///\n906\t    /// * `Ok(())` - Configuration is valid\n907\t    /// * `Err(String)` - Configuration has conflicting options\n908\t    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n909\t        // Check for mutually exclusive options\n910\t        if self.mode.is_strict() && !self.require_all_fields {\n911\t            return Err(\"Strict mode requires require_all_fields to be true\".to_string());\n912\t        }\n913\t\n914\t        if self.mode.is_strict() && !self.disallow_unknown_fields {\n915\t            return Err(\"Strict mode requires disallow_unknown_fields to be true\".to_string());\n916\t        }\n917\t\n918\t        if self.warnings_as_errors && !self.emit_warnings {\n919\t            return Err(\"warnings_as_errors requires emit_warnings to be true\".to_string());\n920\t        }\n921\t\n922\t        Ok(())\n923\t    }\n924\t}\n925\t\n926\t/// Builder for constructing ValidatorConfig instances\n927\t///\n928\t/// Provides a fluent interface for creating validator configurations.\n929\t/// Use [`ValidatorConfig::builder()`] to create an instance.\n930\t///\n931\t/// # Examples\n932\t///\n933\t/// ```\n934\t/// use armor::parsers::config::{ValidatorConfig, ValidationMode};\n935\t///\n936\t/// let config = ValidatorConfig::builder()\n937\t///     .mode(ValidationMode::Strict)\n938\t///     .require_all_fields(true)\n939\t///     .disallow_unknown_fields(true)\n940\t///     .type_checking(true)\n941\t///     .build();\n942\t/// ```\n943\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n944\tpub struct ValidatorConfigBuilder {\n945\t    config: ValidatorConfig,\n946\t}\n947\t\n948\timpl ValidatorConfigBuilder {\n949\t    /// Create a new builder with default configuration\n950\t    fn new() -> Self {\n951\t        Self {\n952\t            config: ValidatorConfig::default(),\n953\t        }\n954\t    }\n955\t\n956\t    /// Set the validation mode\n957\t    pub fn mode(mut self, mode: ValidationMode) -> Self {\n958\t        self.config.mode = mode;\n959\t        self\n960\t    }\n961\t\n962\t    /// Set whether to require all fields\n963\t    pub fn require_all_fields(mut self, require: bool) -> Self {\n964\t        self.config.require_all_fields = require;\n965\t        self\n966\t    }\n967\t\n968\t    /// Set whether to disallow unknown fields\n969\t    pub fn disallow_unknown_fields(mut self, disallow: bool) -> Self {\n970\t        self.config.disallow_unknown_fields = disallow;\n971\t        self\n972\t    }\n973\t\n974\t    /// Set type checking\n975\t    pub fn type_checking(mut self, enabled: bool) -> Self {\n976\t        self.config.type_checking = enabled;\n977\t        self\n978\t    }\n979\t\n980\t    /// Set deep /// Set whether to emit warnings\n987\t    pub fn emit_warnings(mut self, emit: bool) -> Self {\n988\t        self.config.emit_warnings = emit;\n989\t        self\n990\t    }\n991\t\n992\t    /// Set whether to treat warnings as errors\n993\t    pub fn warnings_as_errors(mut self, treat_as_errors: bool) -> Self {\n994\t        self.config.warnings_as_errors = treat_as_errors;\n995\t        self\n996\t    }\n997\t\n998\t    /// Build the final ValidatorConfig\n999\t    ///\n1000\t    /// This method validates the configuration before returning it.\n1001\t    ///\n1002\t    /// # Returns\n1003\t    ///\n1004\t    /// * `Ok(ValidatorConfig)` - Valid configuration\n1005\t    /// * `Err(String)` - Configuration validation failed\n1006\t    pub fn build(self) -> Result<ValidatorConfig, String> {\n1007\t        self.config.validate()?;\n1008\t        Ok(self.config)\n1009\t    }\n1010\t\n1011\t    /// Build without validation (internal use only)\n1012\t    #[doc(hidden)]\n1013\t    pub fn build_unchecked(self) -> ValidatorConfig {\n1014\t        self.config\n1015\t    }\n1016\t}\n1017\t\n1018\t/// Create default parser configuration\n1019\t///\n1020\t/// This is a convenience function that creates a `ParserConfig` with default values.\n1021\t/// Equivalent to `ParserConfig::default()`.\n1022\t///\n1023\t/// # Examples\n1024\t///\n1025\t/// ```\n1026\t/// use armor::parsers::config::default_parser_config;\n1027\t///\n1028\t/// let config = default_parser_config();\n1029\t/// assert!(config.is_lenient());\n1030\t/// ```\n1031\tpub fn default_parser_config() -> ParserConfig {\n1032\t    ParserConfig::default()\n1033\t}\n1034\t\n1035\t/// Create default validator test_parser_config_validate() {\n1201\t        let config = ParserConfig::default();\n1202\t        assert!(config.validate().is_ok());\n1203\t\n1204\t        let strict_config = ParserConfig::strict();\n1205\t        assert!(strict_config.validate().is_ok());\n1206\t    }\n1207\t\n1208\t    #[test]\n1209\t    fn test_parser_config_validate_errors() {\n1210\t        //  }\n1250\t\n1251\t    #[test]\n1252\t    fn test_validation_mode_checks() {\n1253\t        assert!(ValidationMode::Strict.is_strict());\n1254\t        assert!(!ValidationMode::Strict.is_permissive());\n1255\t        assert!(ValidationMode::Permissive.is_permissive());\n1256\t        assert!(!ValidationMode::Permissive.is_strict());\n1257\t    }\n1258\t\n1259\t    #[test]\n1260\t    fn test_validation_mode_default() {\n1261\t        assert_eq!(ValidationMode::default(), ValidationMode::Permissive);\n1262\t    }\n1263\t\n1264\t   assert!(config.emit_warnings);\n1273\t        assert!(!config.warnings_as_errors);\n1274\t    }\n1275\t\n1276\t    #[test]\n1277\t    fn test_validator_config_strict() {\n1278\t        let config = Parser configuration module\n//!\n//! This module provides comprehensive configuration options for controlling\n//! parsing behavior across different parser implementations. It defines:\n//!\n//! - [`ParserConfig`] - Main configuration struct for all parsing options\n//! - [`ParserMode`] - Strict vs lenient parsing modes\n//! - [`TypeConstructor`] - Custom hooks for type construction\n//! - Builder pattern for fluent configuration\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Parser execution mode\n///\n/// Defines the strictness level for parsing operations. Each mode has\n/// specific behaviors for handling malformed input, unknown fields, and\n/// type mismatches.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum ParserMode {\n    /// Strict mode - reject any malformed or unexpected input\n    ///\n    /// In strict mode:\n    /// - Unknown fields cause parsing to fail\n    /// - Type mismatches are errors (not coerced)\n    /// - Duplicate keys are rejected\n    /// - All syntax rules are enforced\n    /// - Missing required fields cause errors\n    Strict,\n\n    /// Lenient mode - attempt to recover from errors\n    ///\n    /// In lenient mode:\n    /// - Unknown fields are silently ignored\n    /// - Type mismatches are coerced when possible (e.g., string → number)\n    /// - Last duplicate key wins (with warning if configured)\n    /// - Some syntax variations are accepted\n    /// - Missing optional fields use defaults\n    Lenient,\n}\n\nimpl ParserMode {\n    /// Check if this mode is strict\n    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Strict)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this mode is lenient\n    pub fn is_lenient(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Lenient)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ParserMode {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Strict => write!(f, \"strict\"),\n            Self::Lenient => write!(f, \"lenient\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ParserMode {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        // Default to lenient mode for better user experience\n        Self::Lenient\n    }\n}\n\n/// Type constructor function signature\n///\n/// This function type defines the signature for custom type constructors.\n/// It takes a field name and raw value, and attempts to construct the\n/// target type from those inputs.\npub type TypeConstructorFn = fn(&str, &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String>;\n\n/// Custom type constructor hook\n///\n/// Allows users to register custom logic for constructing specific types\n/// during parsing. This is useful for:\n///\n/// - Custom enum parsing (e.g., \"warn\" → LogLevel::Warning)\n/// - Validation-rich construction (e.g., ensure ports are in range)\n/// - Complex type assembly (e.g., Duration from \"5s\" string)\n/// - Default value injection for optional fields\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::TypeConstructor;\n///\n/// // Custom constructor for log levels\n/// fn log_level_constructor(\n///     field: &str,\n///     value: &serde_yaml::Value\n/// ) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String> {\n///     let s = value.as_str()\n///         .ok_or(\"expected string\")?\n///         .to_lowercase();\n///\n///     let level = match s.as_str() {\n///         \"debug\" => 0,\n///         \"info\" => 1,\n///         \"warn\" | \"warning\" => 2,\n///         \"error\" => 3,\n///         _ => return Err(format!(\"invalid log level: {}\", s)),\n///     };\n///\n///     Ok(serde_yaml::Value::Number(level.into()))\n/// }\n///\n/// let constructor = TypeConstructor::new(\"LogLevel\", log_level_constructor);\n/// ```\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct TypeConstructor {\n    /// Name of the type this constructor handles (for debugging)\n    pub type_name: String,\n    /// The constructor function\n    pub constructor: TypeConstructorFn,\n}\n\nimpl TypeConstructor {\n    /// Create a new type constructor\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `type_name` - Human-readable name for the type being constructed\n    /// * `constructor` - Function that implements the construction logic\n    pub fn new(type_name: impl Into<String>, constructor: TypeConstructorFn) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            type_name: type_name.into(),\n            constructor,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Invoke the constructor\n    ///\n    /// Calls the underlying constructor function with the provided inputs.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `field` - Field name being constructed\n    /// * `value` - Raw value from the parser\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(serde_yaml::Value)` - Successfully constructed value\n    /// * `Err(String)` - Construction error message\n    pub fn construct(&self, field: &str, value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String> {\n        (self.constructor)(field, value)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Debug for TypeConstructor {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"TypeConstructor\")\n            .field(\"type_name\", &self.type_name)\n            .field(\"constructor\", &\"<function>\")\n            .finish()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Validation hook function signature\n///\n/// This function type defines the signature for custom validation hooks.\n/// It takes a field name and parsed value, and validates that the value\n/// meets application-specific constraints.\npub type ValidationFn = fn(&str, &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<(), String>;\n\n/// Custom validation hook\n///\n/// Allows users to register custom validation logic for specific fields\n/// or types. Validation runs after parsing but before the final result\n/// is returned.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::ValidationHook;\n///\n/// // Validate port numbers are in valid range\n/// fn validate_port(\n///     field: &str,\n///     value: &serde_yaml::Value\n/// ) -> Result<(), String> {\n///     let port = value.as_i64()\n///         .ok_or(\"port must be an integer\")?;\n///\n///     if !(1..=65535).contains(&port) {\n///         return Err(format!(\"port {} out of valid range (1-65535)\", port));\n///     }\n///\n///     Ok(())\n/// }\n///\n/// let hook = ValidationHook::new(\"port\", validate_port);\n/// ```\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct ValidationHook {\n    /// Pattern for matching field names (supports simple \"*\"\" wildcard)\n    pub field_pattern: String,\n    /// The validation function\n    pub validator: ValidationFn,\n}\n\nimpl ValidationHook {\n    /// Create a new validation hook\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `field_pattern` - Pattern to match field names (supports \"*\"\" wildcard)\n    /// * `validator` - Function that implements the validation logic\n    pub fn new(field_pattern: impl Into<String>, validator: ValidationFn) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            field_pattern: field_pattern.into(),\n            validator,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this hook applies to the given field\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `field` - Field name to check\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the field pattern matches, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn applies_to(&self, field: &str) -> bool {\n        if self.field_pattern == \"*\" {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        if self.field_pattern.ends_with('*') {\n            let prefix = &self.field_pattern[..self.field_pattern.len() - 1];\n            return field.starts_with(prefix);\n        }\n\n        self.field_pattern == field\n    }\n\n    /// Invoke the validator\n    ///\n    /// Calls the underlying validation function with the provided inputs.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `field` - Field name being validated\n    /// * `value` - Parsed value to validate\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(())` - Validation passed\n    /// * `Err(String)` - Validation error message\n    pub fn validate(&self, field: &str, value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<(), String> {\n        (self.validator)(field, value)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Debug for ValidationHook {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"ValidationHook\")\n            .field(\"field_pattern\", &self.field_pattern)\n            .field(\"validator\", &\"<function>\")\n            .finish()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Comprehensive parser configuration\n///\n/// This structure consolidates all configuration options for parsing behavior.\n/// It provides fine-grained control over parsing, validation, and type construction.\n///\n/// # Default Configuration\n///\n/// The default configuration is:\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// ParserConfig {\n///     mode: Lenient,\n///     allow_duplicates: true,\n///     preserve_comments: false,\n///     preserve_quotes: false,\n///     max_depth: 0,  // unlimited\n///     strict_types: false,\n///     .. }\n/// ```\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ## Using Defaults\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n///\n/// let config = ParserConfig::default();\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Strict Mode\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n///\n/// let config = ParserConfig::strict();\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Builder Pattern\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, ParserMode};\n///\n/// let config = ParserConfig::builder()\n///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n///     .allow_duplicates(false)\n///     .max_depth(10)\n///     .build();\n/// ```\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ParserConfig {\n    /// Parsing mode (strict vs lenient)\n    pub mode: ParserMode,\n    /// Allow duplicate keys in mappings\n    pub allow_duplicates: bool,\n    /// Preserve comments in the output (if format supports it)\n    pub preserve_comments: bool,\n    /// Preserve quote information in parsed strings\n    pub preserve_quotes: bool,\n    /// Preserve order of mapping keys (use index-based map instead of hash map)\n    pub preserve_order: bool,\n    /// Maximum nesting depth (0 = unlimited)\n    pub max_depth: usize,\n    /// Enforce strict type checking (no implicit coercion)\n    pub strict_types: bool,\n    /// Custom type constructors registered by field name\n    pub type_constructors: HashMap<String, TypeConstructor>,\n    /// Custom validation hooks\n    pub validation_hooks: Vec<ValidationHook>,\n    /// Emit warnings for recoverable errors\n    pub emit_warnings: bool,\n    /// Treat warnings as errors (fail on warnings)\n    pub warnings_as_errors: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for ParserConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            mode: ParserMode::default(),\n            allow_duplicates: true,\n            preserve_comments: false,\n            preserve_quotes: false,\n            preserve_order: false,\n            max_depth: 0,\n            strict_types: false,\n            type_constructors: HashMap::new(),\n            validation_hooks: Vec::new(),\n            emit_warnings: false,\n            warnings_as_errors: false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ParserConfig {\n    /// Create a builder for constructing ParserConfig\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, ParserMode};\n    ///\n    /// let config = ParserConfig::builder()\n    ///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n    ///     .build();\n    /// ```\n    pub fn builder() -> ParserConfigBuilder {\n        ParserConfigBuilder::new()\n    }\n\n    /// Create a strict-mode configuration\n    ///\n    /// This is a convenience method that creates a configuration optimized\n    /// for strict validation. Equivalent to:\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// ParserConfig::builder()\n    ///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n    ///     .allow_duplicates(false)\n    ///     .strict_types(true)\n    ///     .build()\n    /// ```\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n    ///\n    /// let config = ParserConfig::strict();\n    /// assert!(config.mode.is_strict());\n    /// assert!(!config.allow_duplicates);\n    /// assert!(config.strict_types);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            mode: ParserMode::Strict,\n            allow_duplicates: false,\n            strict_types: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a lenient-mode configuration\n    ///\n    /// This is a convenience method that creates a configuration optimized\n    /// for forgiving parsing. Equivalent to:\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// ParserConfig::builder()\n    ///     .mode(ParserMode::Lenient)\n    ///     .allow_duplicates(true)\n    ///     .strict_types(false)\n    ///     .build()\n    /// ```\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::config::ParserConfig;\n    ///\n    /// let config = ParserConfig::lenient();\n    /// assert!(config.mode.is_lenient());\n    /// assert!(config.allow_duplicates);\n    /// assert!(!config.strict_types);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn lenient() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            mode: ParserMode::Lenient,\n            allow_duplicates: true,\n            strict_types: false,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if strict mode is enabled\n    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool {\n        self.mode.is_strict()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if lenient mode is enabled\n    pub fn is_lenient(&self) -> bool {\n        self.mode.is_lenient()\n    }\n\n    /// Register a custom type constructor\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `field` - Field name this constructor handles\n    /// * `constructor` - The type constructor to register\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, TypeConstructor};\n    ///\n    /// fn make_duration(_: &str, v: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value, String> {\n    ///     // Implementation...\n    ///     Ok(v.clone())\n    /// }\n    ///\n    /// let mut config = ParserConfig::default();\n    /// config.register_constructor(\"timeout\", TypeConstructor::new(\"Duration\", make_duration));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn register_constructor(&mut self, field: impl Into<String>, constructor: TypeConstructor) {\n        self.type_constructors.insert(field.into(), constructor);\n    }\n\n    /// Register a custom validation hook\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `hook` - The validation hook to register\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, ValidationHook};\n    ///\n    /// fn validate_port(field: &str, v: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<(), String> {\n    ///     // Implementation...\n    ///     Ok(())\n    /// }\n    ///\n    /// let mut config = ParserConfig::default();\n    /// config.register_validation(ValidationHook::new(\"port\", validate_port));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn register_validation(&mut self, hook: ValidationHook) {\n        self.validation_hooks.push(hook);\n    }\n\n    /// Get type constructor for a field (if registered)\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `field` - Field name to look up\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Some(&TypeConstructor)` if registered, `None` otherwise\n    pub fn get_constructor(&self, field: &str) -> Option<&TypeConstructor> {\n        self.type_constructors.get(field)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all applicable validation hooks for a field\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `field` - Field name to check\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// Iterator over all validation hooks that apply to this field\n    pub fn get_validations<'a>(&'a self, field: &'a str) -> impl Iterator<Item = &'a ValidationHook> {\n        self.validation_hooks\n            .iter()\n            .filter(move |hook| hook.applies_to(field))\n    }\n\n    /// Validate configuration for mutually exclusive or inconsistent options\n    ///\n    /// This method checks if any mutually exclusive options are set together\n    /// or if the configuration has inconsistent settings. Returns an error if\n    /// validation fails.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(())` - Configuration is valid\n    /// * `Err(String)` - Configuration has conflicting or inconsistent options\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n        // Check for mutually exclusive or inconsistent options\n        if self.warnings_as_errors && !self.emit_warnings {\n            return Err(\"warnings_as_errors requires emit_warnings to be true\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        // Strict mode should not allow duplicates\n        if self.mode.is_strict() && self.allow_duplicates {\n            return Err(\"Strict mode with allow_duplicates=true is inconsistent\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        // Strict types should align with strict mode\n        if self.strict_types && self.mode.is_lenient() {\n            return Err(\"strict_types=true with lenient mode is inconsistent\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Builder for constructing ParserConfig instances\n///\n/// Provides a fluent interface for creating parser configurations.\n/// Use [`ParserConfig::builder()`] to create an instance.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::{ParserConfig, ParserMode};\n///\n/// let config = ParserConfig::builder()\n///     .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n///     .allow_duplicates(false)\n///     .max_depth(10)\n///     .preserve_comments(true)\n///     .build();\n/// ```\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ParserConfigBuilder {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n}\n\nimpl ParserConfigBuilder {\n    /// Create a new builder with default configuration\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the parsing mode\n    pub fn mode(mut self, mode: ParserMode) -> Self {\n        self.config.mode = mode;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to allow duplicate keys\n    pub fn allow_duplicates(mut self, allow: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.allow_duplicates = allow;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to preserve comments\n    pub fn preserve_comments(mut self, preserve: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.preserve_comments = preserve;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to preserve quote information\n    pub fn preserve_quotes(mut self, preserve: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.preserve_quotes = preserve;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to preserve mapping key order\n    pub fn preserve_order(mut self, preserve: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.preserve_order = preserve;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set maximum nesting depth\n    pub fn max_depth(mut self, depth: usize) -> Self {\n        self.config.max_depth = depth;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set strict type checking\n    pub fn strict_types(mut self, strict: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.strict_types = strict;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Add a type constructor\n    pub fn with_constructor(mut self, field: impl Into<String>, constructor: TypeConstructor) -> Self {\n        self.config.register_constructor(field, constructor);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Add a validation hook\n    pub fn with_validation(mut self, hook: ValidationHook) -> Self {\n        self.config.register_validation(hook);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to emit warnings\n    pub fn emit_warnings(mut self, emit: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.emit_warnings = emit;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to treat warnings as errors\n    pub fn warnings_as_errors(mut self, treat_as_errors: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.warnings_as_errors = treat_as_errors;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Build the final ParserConfig\n    ///\n    /// This method validates the configuration before returning it.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(ParserConfig)` - Valid configuration\n    /// * `Err(String)` - Configuration validation failed\n    pub fn build(self) -> Result<ParserConfig, String> {\n        self.config.validate()?;\n        Ok(self.config)\n    }\n\n    /// Build without validation (internal use only)\n    #[doc(hidden)]\n    pub fn build_unchecked(self) -> ParserConfig {\n        self.config\n    }\n}\n\n/// Validation mode for field checking\n///\n/// Defines the strictness level for validation operations. Each mode has\n/// specific behaviors for field presence and type checking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum ValidationMode {\n    /// Strict mode - enforce all validation rules\n    ///\n    /// In strict mode:\n    /// - All required fields must be present\n    /// - Unknown fields cause validation to fail\n    /// - Type mismatches are errors\n    Strict,\n\n    /// Permissive mode - allow some flexibility\n    ///\n    /// In permissive mode:\n    /// - Required fields use defaults if missing\n    /// - Unknown fields are ignored with warnings\n    /// - Type mismatches are coerced when possible\n    Permissive,\n}\n\nimpl ValidationMode {\n    /// Check if this mode is strict\n    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Strict)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this mode is permissive\n    pub fn is_permissive(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Permissive)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ValidationMode {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Strict => write!(f, \"strict\"),\n            Self::Permissive => write!(f, \"permissive\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ValidationMode {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::Permissive\n    }\n}\n\n/// Comprehensive validator configuration\n///\n/// This structure consolidates all configuration options for validation behavior.\n/// It provides fine-grained control over field checking, type validation, and\n/// constraint enforcement.\n///\n/// # Default Configuration\n///\n/// The default configuration is:\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// ValidatorConfig {\n///     mode: Permissive,\n///     require_all_fields: false,\n///     disallow_unknown_fields: false,\n///     type_checking: true,\n///     .. }\n/// ```\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ## Using Defaults\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::ValidatorConfig;\n///\n/// let config = ValidatorConfig::default();\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Strict Mode\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::ValidatorConfig;\n///\n/// let config = ValidatorConfig::strict();\n/// ```\n///\n/// ## Builder Pattern\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// use armor::parsers::config::{ValidatorConfig, ValidationMode};\n///\n/// let config = ValidatorConfig::builder()\n///     .mode(ValidationMode::Strict)\n///     .require_all_fields(true)\n///     .disallow_unknown_fields(true)\n///     .build();\n/// ```\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ValidatorConfig {\n    /// Validation mode (strict vs permissive)\n    pub mode: ValidationMode,\n    /// Require all fields to be present (no missing fields)\n    pub require_all_fields: bool,\n    /// Disallow unknown fields (fail on unexpected fields)\n    pub disallow_unknown_fields: bool,\n    /// Enable type checking (validate field types match schema)\n    pub type_checking: bool,\n    /// Perform deep validation on nested structures\n    pub deep_validation: bool,\n    /// Emit warnings for non-critical issues\n    pub emit_warnings: bool,\n    /// Treat warnings as errors (fail on warnings)\n    pub warnings_as_errors: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for ValidatorConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            mode: ValidationMode::default(),\n            require_all_fields: false,\n            disallow_unknown_fields: false,\n            type_checking: true,\n            deep_validation: true,\n            emit_warnings: true,\n            warnings_as_errors: false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ValidatorConfig {\n    /// Create a builder for constructing ValidatorConfig\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::config::{ValidatorConfig, ValidationMode};\n    ///\n    /// let config = ValidatorConfig::builder()\n    ///     .mode(ValidationMode::Strict)\n    ///     .build();\n    /// ```\n    pub fn builder() -> ValidatorConfigBuilder {\n        ValidatorConfigBuilder::new()\n    }\n\n    /// Create a strict-mode configuration\n    ///\n    /// This is a convenience method that creates a configuration optimized\n    /// for strict validation. Equivalent to:\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// ValidatorConfig::builder()\n    ///     .mode(ValidationMode::Strict)\n    ///     .require_all_fields(true)\n    ///     .disallow_unknown_fields(true)\n    ///     .type_checking(true)\n    ///     .build()\n    /// ```\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::config::ValidatorConfig;\n    ///\n    /// let config = ValidatorConfig::strict();\n    /// assert!(config.mode.is_strict());\n    /// assert!(config.require_all_fields);\n    /// assert!(config.disallow_unknown_fields);\n    /// assert!(config.type_checking);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            mode: ValidationMode::Strict,\n            require_all_fields: true,\n            disallow_unknown_fields: true,\n            type_checking: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a permissive-mode configuration\n    ///\n    /// This is a convenience method that creates a configuration optimized\n    /// for flexible validation. Equivalent to:\n    ///\n    /// ```ignore\n    /// ValidatorConfig::builder()\n    ///     .mode(ValidationMode::Permissive)\n    ///     .require_all_fields(false)\n    ///     .disallow_unknown_fields(false)\n    ///     .type_checking(true)\n    ///     .build()\n    /// ```\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::config::ValidatorConfig;\n    ///\n    /// let config = ValidatorConfig::permissive();\n    /// assert!(config.mode.is_permissive());\n    /// assert!(!config.require_all_fields);\n    /// assert!(!config.disallow_unknown_fields);\n    /// assert!(config.type_checking);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn permissive() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            mode: ValidationMode::Permissive,\n            require_all_fields: false,\n            disallow_unknown_fields: false,\n            type_checking: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if strict mode is enabled\n    pub fn is_strict(&self) -> bool {\n        self.mode.is_strict()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if permissive mode is enabled\n    pub fn is_permissive(&self) -> bool {\n        self.mode.is_permissive()\n    }\n\n    /// Validate configuration for mutually exclusive options\n    ///\n    /// This method checks if any mutually exclusive options are set together.\n    /// Returns an error if validation fails.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(())` - Configuration is valid\n    /// * `Err(String)` - Configuration has conflicting options\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), String> {\n        // Check for mutually exclusive options\n        if self.mode.is_strict() && !self.require_all_fields {\n            return Err(\"Strict mode requires require_all_fields to be true\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        if self.mode.is_strict() && !self.disallow_unknown_fields {\n            return Err(\"Strict mode requires disallow_unknown_fields to be true\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        if self.warnings_as_errors && !self.emit_warnings {\n            return Err(\"warnings_as_errors requires emit_warnings to be true\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Builder for constructing ValidatorConfig instances\n///\n/// Provides a fluent interface for creating validator configurations.\n/// Use [`ValidatorConfig::builder()`] to create an instance.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::{ValidatorConfig, ValidationMode};\n///\n/// let config = ValidatorConfig::builder()\n///     .mode(ValidationMode::Strict)\n///     .require_all_fields(true)\n///     .disallow_unknown_fields(true)\n///     .type_checking(true)\n///     .build();\n/// ```\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ValidatorConfigBuilder {\n    config: ValidatorConfig,\n}\n\nimpl ValidatorConfigBuilder {\n    /// Create a new builder with default configuration\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ValidatorConfig::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the validation mode\n    pub fn mode(mut self, mode: ValidationMode) -> Self {\n        self.config.mode = mode;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to require all fields\n    pub fn require_all_fields(mut self, require: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.require_all_fields = require;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to disallow unknown fields\n    pub fn disallow_unknown_fields(mut self, disallow: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.disallow_unknown_fields = disallow;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set type checking\n    pub fn type_checking(mut self, enabled: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.type_checking = enabled;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set deep validation on nested structures\n    pub fn deep_validation(mut self, enabled: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.deep_validation = enabled;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to emit warnings\n    pub fn emit_warnings(mut self, emit: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.emit_warnings = emit;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether to treat warnings as errors\n    pub fn warnings_as_errors(mut self, treat_as_errors: bool) -> Self {\n        self.config.warnings_as_errors = treat_as_errors;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Build the final ValidatorConfig\n    ///\n    /// This method validates the configuration before returning it.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// * `Ok(ValidatorConfig)` - Valid configuration\n    /// * `Err(String)` - Configuration validation failed\n    pub fn build(self) -> Result<ValidatorConfig, String> {\n        self.config.validate()?;\n        Ok(self.config)\n    }\n\n    /// Build without validation (internal use only)\n    #[doc(hidden)]\n    pub fn build_unchecked(self) -> ValidatorConfig {\n        self.config\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create default parser configuration\n///\n/// This is a convenience function that creates a `ParserConfig` with default values.\n/// Equivalent to `ParserConfig::default()`.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::default_parser_config;\n///\n/// let config = default_parser_config();\n/// assert!(config.is_lenient());\n/// ```\npub fn default_parser_config() -> ParserConfig {\n    ParserConfig::default()\n}\n\n/// Create default validator configuration\n///\n/// This is a convenience function that creates a `ValidatorConfig` with default values.\n/// Equivalent to `ValidatorConfig::default()`.\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::config::default_validator_config;\n///\n/// let config = default_validator_config();\n/// assert!(config.is_permissive());\n/// ```\npub fn default_validator_config() -> ValidatorConfig {\n    ValidatorConfig::default()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use serde_yaml::Value;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_mode_display() {\n        assert_eq!(ParserMode::Strict.to_string(), \"strict\");\n        assert_eq!(ParserMode::Lenient.to_string(), \"lenient\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_mode_checks() {\n        assert!(ParserMode::Strict.is_strict());\n        assert!(!ParserMode::Strict.is_lenient());\n        assert!(ParserMode::Lenient.is_lenient());\n        assert!(!ParserMode::Lenient.is_strict());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_mode_default() {\n        assert_eq!(ParserMode::default(), ParserMode::Lenient);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_type_constructor() {\n        fn simple_constructor(_: &str, value: &Value) -> Result<Value, String> {\n            Ok(value.clone())\n        }\n\n        let constructor = TypeConstructor::new(\"TestType\", simple_constructor);\n        assert_eq!(constructor.type_name, \"TestType\");\n\n        let input = Value::Number(42.into());\n        let result = constructor.construct(\"field\", &input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result, input);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validation_hook_pattern_matching() {\n        fn noop(_: &str, _: &Value) -> Result<(), String> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        let hook = ValidationHook::new(\"port_*\", noop);\n        assert!(hook.applies_to(\"port_http\"));\n        assert!(hook.applies_to(\"port_https\"));\n        assert!(!hook.applies_to(\"timeout\"));\n\n        let universal_hook = ValidationHook::new(\"*\", noop);\n        assert!(universal_hook.applies_to(\"any_field\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_default() {\n        let config = ParserConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.is_lenient());\n        assert!(config.allow_duplicates);\n        assert!(!config.preserve_comments);\n        assert!(!config.preserve_quotes);\n        assert!(!config.preserve_order);\n        assert_eq!(config.max_depth, 0);\n        assert!(!config.strict_types);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_strict() {\n        let config = ParserConfig::strict();\n        assert!(config.is_strict());\n        assert!(!config.allow_duplicates);\n        assert!(config.strict_types);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_lenient() {\n        let config = ParserConfig::lenient();\n        assert!(config.is_lenient());\n        assert!(config.allow_duplicates);\n        assert!(!config.strict_types);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_register_constructor() {\n        fn dummy_constructor(_: &str, _: &Value) -> Result<Value, String> {\n            Ok(Value::Null)\n        }\n\n        let mut config = ParserConfig::default();\n        config.register_constructor(\"timeout\", TypeConstructor::new(\"Duration\", dummy_constructor));\n\n        assert!(config.get_constructor(\"timeout\").is_some());\n        assert!(config.get_constructor(\"unknown\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_register_validation() {\n        fn dummy_validation(_: &str, _: &Value) -> Result<(), String> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        let mut config = ParserConfig::default();\n        config.register_validation(ValidationHook::new(\"port_*\", dummy_validation));\n\n        let hooks: Vec<_> = config.get_validations(\"port_http\").collect();\n        assert_eq!(hooks.len(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_builder() {\n        let config = ParserConfig::builder()\n            .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n            .allow_duplicates(false)\n            .max_depth(10)\n            .preserve_comments(true)\n            .preserve_order(true)\n            .strict_types(true)\n            .build()\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert!(config.is_strict());\n        assert!(!config.allow_duplicates);\n        assert_eq!(config.max_depth, 10);\n        assert!(config.preserve_comments);\n        assert!(config.preserve_order);\n        assert!(config.strict_types);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_builder_with_hooks() {\n        fn constructor(_: &str, v: &Value) -> Result<Value, String> {\n            Ok(v.clone())\n        }\n\n        fn validation(_: &str, _: &Value) -> Result<(), String> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        let config = ParserConfig::builder()\n            .with_constructor(\"timeout\", TypeConstructor::new(\"Duration\", constructor))\n            .with_validation(ValidationHook::new(\"port\", validation))\n            .build()\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert!(config.get_constructor(\"timeout\").is_some());\n        assert_eq!(config.get_validations(\"port\").count(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_validate() {\n        let config = ParserConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.validate().is_ok());\n\n        let strict_config = ParserConfig::strict();\n        assert!(strict_config.validate().is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_validate_errors() {\n        // warnings_as_errors without emit_warnings should fail\n        let config = ParserConfig {\n            emit_warnings: false,\n            warnings_as_errors: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert!(config.validate().is_err());\n\n        // Strict mode with allow_duplicates should fail\n        let config2 = ParserConfig {\n            mode: ParserMode::Strict,\n            allow_duplicates: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert!(config2.validate().is_err());\n\n        // strict_types with lenient mode should fail\n        let config3 = ParserConfig {\n            mode: ParserMode::Lenient,\n            strict_types: true,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        assert!(config3.validate().is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parser_config_builder_validation_error() {\n        let result = ParserConfig::builder()\n            .mode(ParserMode::Strict)\n            .allow_duplicates(true)  // Invalid for strict mode\n            .build();\n\n        assert!(result.is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validation_mode_display() {\n        assert_eq!(ValidationMode::Strict.to_string(), \"strict\");\n        assert_eq!(ValidationMode::Permissive.to_string(), \"permissive\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validation_mode_checks() {\n        assert!(ValidationMode::Strict.is_strict());\n        assert!(!ValidationMode::Strict.is_permissive());\n        assert!(ValidationMode::Permissive.is_permissive());\n        assert!(!ValidationMode::Permissive.is_strict());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validation_mode_default() {\n        assert_eq!(ValidationMode::default(), ValidationMode::Permissive);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validator_config_default() {\n        let config = ValidatorConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.is_permissive());\n        assert!(!config.require_all_fields);\n        assert!(!config.disallow_unknown_fields);\n        assert!(config.type_checking);\n        assert!(config.deep_validation);\n        assert!(config.emit_warnings);\n        assert!(!config.warnings_as_errors);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validator_config_strict() {\n        let assert!(config.type_checking);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validator_config_permissive() {\n        let config = ValidatorConfig::permissive();\n        assert!(config.is_permissive());\n ValidationError},\n    ParserConfig,\n    syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator,\n    syntax_detector::SyntaxDetector,\n};\n\n/// Trait for YAML parsers\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsing YAML content.\n/// Implementations can parse from strings, files, or other sources.\npub trait Parser {\n    /// Parse YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ParseResult containing the parsed data or an error\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a byte slice\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a file\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Validate a YAML file without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig;\n\n    /// Set the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parser with the new configuration\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized;\n}\n\n/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n///\n/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n}\n\nimpl BasicParser {\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n        Self { config }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new strict parser\n    ///\n    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for BasicParser {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Parser for BasicParser {\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Stub implementation\n        ParseResult::success(serde_yaml::Value::Null)\n    }\n\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Stub implementation\n        ParseResult::success(serde_yaml::Value::Null)\n    }\n\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Stub implementation\n        ParseResult::success(serde_yaml::Value::Null)\n    }\n\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n        } else {\n            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n        };\n\n        // Run syntax validation\n        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n\n        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n        if result.is_valid() {\n            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n\n            // Merge errors from detector\n            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n
